developer.name: James Carmichael
developer.title: Psychology Major & Aspiring Criminal Defense Attorney
developer.tagline: Full Ride Scholarship | Future Law School Candidate
developer.bio: A psychology major at Kean University with a passion for understanding the human mind and defending those who need it most. After completing my degree on a full ride scholarship, I plan to pursue law school and become a criminal defense attorney. My background in web development has taught me problem-solving, attention to detail, and the discipline to master complex systems — skills that will serve me well in law.

projects.title: HTML Foundations Blog
projects.description: My very first web project — a personal blog built from scratch using pure HTML and CSS. Learned the fundamentals of web structure and visual design.
projects.reflection: This was where it all began. I remember staring at a blank HTML file wondering what to type. By the end, I had something I was genuinely proud of.

projects.title: Responsive Photography Portfolio
projects.description: A responsive photography showcase site built with CSS Grid and Flexbox. My first serious attempt at mobile-first design.
projects.reflection: Learning that websites needed to work on phones changed everything for me. Responsive design felt like a superpower.

projects.title: JavaScript Calculator
projects.description: An interactive calculator app — my introduction to JavaScript programming and DOM manipulation.
projects.reflection: The moment my calculator worked for the first time, I felt like a real programmer. It was magical.

projects.title: Weather Dashboard App
projects.description: A real-time weather application using the OpenWeather API. My first experience with async JavaScript and API integration.
projects.reflection: Working with external APIs for the first time felt like unlocking a cheat code. Suddenly my apps could talk to the real world.

projects.title: Vue.js Task Manager
projects.description: A full-featured task management app built with Vue.js — complete with filtering, categories, and local storage persistence.
projects.reflection: Vue.js completely changed how I thought about building interfaces. Reactive data felt like magic at first.

projects.title: E-Commerce Mockup
projects.description: A complete e-commerce UI mockup with product pages, cart functionality, and checkout flow.
projects.reflection: This project taught me the importance of UX thinking. Good design isn't just about looks — it's about how things work.

projects.title: This Portfolio
projects.description: The one you're looking at right now. A Persona 5-inspired interactive portfolio experience built as my senior capstone project.
projects.reflection: Building this felt like everything I'd learned coming together. Every challenge from the past three years prepared me for this moment.

projects.title: Music Visualizer
projects.description: An interactive audio visualizer using the Web Audio API with custom canvas animations.
projects.reflection: Combining my love of music and programming felt deeply personal. This project represents who I am beyond just code.

reflection.overall: Three years in the Web Design Pathway Program taught me discipline, creativity, and problem-solving—skills that have become the foundation for my journey in psychology and my pursuit of criminal law. Web design taught me that understanding user needs is essential, and criminal defense requires understanding the human experiences, fears, and rights of my future clients. Every bug I fixed made me more patient; every client I'll defend will deserve that same patience and dedication.
reflection.challenges: The hardest moments came from imposter syndrome and wrestling with complex problems. But I learned that challenges are just information waiting to be understood. This mindset is exactly what I'll need in criminal law—where understanding human behavior, legal precedent, and the justice system's complexities requires the same problem-solving approach I developed in web design.
reflection.favorites: My favorite memory is the moment I realized I could create something meaningful that affects how people interact with the world. Now, I'm driven by an even deeper purpose: to defend people's constitutional rights and navigate the criminal justice system with compassion and expertise.
reflection.transformation: Web design and psychology have given me a unique perspective: the ability to understand systems (both code and human behavior) and create meaningful change. I'm no longer just building interfaces—I'm preparing to defend justice.

futurePlans.college: Majoring in Psychology at Kean University on a full ride scholarship for 4 years.
futurePlans.career: Planning to attend law school to earn my Juris Doctor degree and become a criminal defense attorney.
goals: Complete my psychology degree with honors
Prepare for and excel on the LSAT
Build a career defending clients' rights
futurePlans.message: My passion is understanding the human mind and using that knowledge to defend those who need it most. Criminal law allows me to combine psychology and advocacy to make a real difference in people's lives during their most difficult moments.
